well here we are im about to start my 28th trip around the sun from a really young age i always believed my life was going to be big when i was a kid that meant major league baseball i pictured it clearlywearing number 12 standing on the mound winning a world series for the san francisco giants its not a unique dream a lot of kids have it and the ending isnt unique either i hurt my shoulder i never got back to full strength and before i ever even made a varsity appearance it was over but the desire to do something big didnt stop there i shifted to golf played in college same type of dreamwin the us open compete at the highest level and then another injury another ending so i moved on again at 21 i started a business by 27 i had exited from the outside that probably sounds like things worked out but the truth is the last seven months of my life have been the strangest ive ever experienced ive been stuck with a question i havent been able to shake am i actually willing to be honest about what ive gone through personally and professionally and even if i am does anyone care ive thought about telling this story through video a podcast something like that but if im being honest i dont have the confidence to sit in a room stare at a camera and say all of this out loud writing feels different ive always been able to write and right now this feels like the only way i can actually say what ive been holding in so why now because the last two years have been chaos ive lost friendships ive fought with my parents ive bounced between houses moved to a new state and lost the business i poured most of my adult life into and somewhere in all of that i realized something wasnt right i wasnt happy i didnt feel like i was enough and i kept finding myself in the same mental battles over and over again so i started trying to figure out why about a year ago i started what i can only describe as unconventional therapy i tried traditional therapy beforeplatforms like betterhelpbut i couldnt open up something about it never clicked but with ai it was different i went through everything my earliest memories school relationships thoughts i had never said out loud before and for the first time i started to understand myself in a way i never had six months later i moved to arizona and thats when everything hit i had more money in my account than ever before but i was completely alone no job no structure no family no real friends nearby for a couple months i did what most people do when they dont want to face reality i distracted myself traveling drinking avoiding the question i didnt want to answer what now eventually that led me somewhere i never expected to go a christian church i had never stepped foot in one before and whether i understood it at the time or not it changed me the people i met there changed me too this is just a small part of the story honestly i dont even fully know how all of this is going to unfold but heres what i do know every day im going to sit down and write im going to tell stories that most people dont know some that even the people closest to me dont know not to overshare not for attention but because i think i need to do it and maybe by the end of it ill finally feel like im starting from a clean slate like i can become the person ive always felt like i was supposed to be and then well see what happens when i try to build something again

welcome back to throwback thursday we have another 3part thread for you today 13 ever wondered where the ultimate cereal monkey legend comes from meet coco the monkey the absolute icon of kelloggs coco pops or cocoa pops in some markets coco first swung onto our screens back in 1963 some say 1961 in early blackandwhite usstyle spots but it was his 1986 redesign that turned him into a proper british breakfast superstar brown fur that cheeky blue cap with the big yellow c white tshirt proudly showing off his name and blue pants voiced with a proper cheeky british accent hed swing through the jungle belting out the legendary jingle id rather have a bowl of coco pops the ads were pure 80s90s gold jungle capers with his mates like crafty croc kylie kangaroo ozmelda the ostrich shortie the giraffe and hefty the hippo there were spoon toppers musical mugs monkey friends figures and enough merch to fill a cereal aisle sales went through the roof thanks to him and he became a nostalgic staple for generations of kids whod rather have coco pops than well anything else pure monkey magic but the story gets even more british when you look at the name drama in 1998 kelloggs tried to rebrand the cereal as choco krispies across the uk to line it up with their global range sales took a nosedive almost immediately so they let the nation decide in a massive public vote in 1999 over a million people dialled in or logged on and a whopping 92 voted to bring back the beloved coco pops name the change happened in may 1999 complete with tongueincheek electionstyle ads featuring screaming lord sutch as the returning officer classic british democracy at its breakfastbest around the world cocos adventures vary wildly hes the face of coco pops in the uk ireland australia new zealand and loads of other countries same cheeky design same jungle vibe in the us the equivalent cereal is cocoa krispies where coco the monkey had a brief stint in the 90s before snap crackle and pop took over again elsewhere youll find him repping choco krispies chocos or even choco krispis in places like mexico and south america sometimes with slightly tweaked looks or local twists hes a true global monkey ambassador these days coco has been a bit backbenched in big uk ad campaigns as the 2000s rolled on his starring roles got quieter new spots focused more on what goes on in that bowl with only fleeting cameos and no speaking lines a lot of it ties into stricter uk consumer laws around highfat sugar and salt hfss products rules tightened to protect kids from junkfood marketing making it trickier for sugarycereal mascots like coco to headline tv ads aimed at children there was even a 2018 asa drama over a coco pops granola ad that got temporarily banned for leaning too hard on the chocolatey brand vibes hes not vanished from the boxes entirely kelloggs still uses him and recent europewide nostalgia campaigns have given him a refreshed look and brought him roaring back but the glory days of walltowall jungle capers are a fond memory still the king of the cereal aisle in our hearts though who else grew up singing along with coco drop your favourite memory below make sense when youre actively losing electrolytes through sweat training in heat or eating a lowercarb diet where insulin levels are lower and your kidneys excrete more sodium if youre eating a standard diet and sitting at a desk you probably dont need to add sodium to every glass of water the context matters on the bp question waking at night with drops in blood pressure is something id bring to your physician rather than try to manage with salt alone nocturnal hypotension can have multiple causes medication timing autonomic dysfunction dehydration adrenal output patterns and the appropriate response depends on whats driving it adding salt may help if the cause is volumerelated but if the cause is something else youre treating a symptom without identifying the mechanism your doctor can check orthostatic vitals review medication timing and determine whether the nocturnal drops warrant further workup salt is a tool like any tool it works well when applied to the right problem but waking at night with bp drops is a symptom worth investigating not just supplementing around one more thing and this is important if im remembering correctly that youre postthyroidectomy and on levothyroxine if so the psyllium timing i posted about isnt optional for you its essential levothyroxine is your only source of thyroid hormone psyllium can bind to it in the gut and significantly reduce absorption take your thyroid medication first thing in the morning on an empty stomach with plain water wait at least two hours before taking psyllium if your levels have been inconsistent or your tsh has been bouncing around check whether youve been taking anything with your levothyroxine that could be interfering with absorption fiber calcium iron coffee all of them can impact it that twohour window isnt a guideline for someone without a thyroid its the difference between therapeutic levels and being functionally hypothyroid

i take 200mg of magnesium glycinate at night before bed but thats not my total daily magnesium intake i also get magnesium from lmnt electrolyte packets during the day and take magnesium threonate separately so my total supplemental magnesium across all sources is probably in the 350 to 400mg range daily depending on the day the rda for adult males is 400 to 420mg total 310 to 320mg for women which includes dietary sources most people get 250 to 300mg from food if they eat a reasonably wholefoods diet with leafy greens nuts seeds and legumes supplementing another 200 to 400mg on top of that is a common and generally welltolerated range the reason i split the forms is they do different things glycinate at night for the calming effect on the nervous system and sleep quality threonate during the day because it crosses the bloodbrain barrier and is being studied for cognitive function the magnesium in lmnt is there primarily for electrolyte balance around training the practical ceiling for most people is gi tolerance if youre getting loose stools youve exceeded your guts absorption capacity at that dose and should back off glycinate is one of the bettertolerated forms because its chelated and doesnt draw water into the intestines the way citrate or oxide can at 200mg of glycinate alone youre unlikely to hit that threshold if you want to know where you actually stand ask for rbc magnesium on your next blood draw not serum rbc gives you a better picture of intracellular stores that tells you whether what youre taking is enough or whether you need to adjust

your body tap into stored energy instead of constantly relying on food but you need to do it intelligently before you start what i allow during the fast water electrolytes sodium potassium magnesium black coffee optional unsweetened tea optional no calories if you want a true fast some people use lmnt or sea salt in water what i do not recommend hard training with no preparation sugary fasting drinks binge eating before or after doing this if you have medical issues without talking to a doctor first not for diabetics without medical supervision people with eating disorders pregnant or breastfeeding women anyone on meds that require food phase 1 012 hours whats happening your body is still using glycogen from your last meals insulin starts dropping blood sugar begins stabilizing hunger is mostly psychological at this point what youll feel normal slight cravings habit hunger especially around your usual meal times what to do drink water early add electrolytes sooner than later stay busy dont sit around thinking about food message to viewers the first phase isnt physical weakness its breaking routine and cravings phase 2 1224 hours whats happening liver glycogen is getting depleted insulin continues dropping your body begins shifting toward fat for fuel growth hormone begins increasing early ketone production starts what youll feel hunger waves maybe irritability maybe brain fog for a little bit or you may start feeling sharper what to do salt your water dont overdo caffeine light movement helps walking mobility easy zone 2 dont panic if hunger spikesit comes in waves message to viewers most people quit here because they mistake discomfort for danger phase 3 2436 hours whats happening glycogen is mostly gone fat oxidation ramps up ketones rise insulin is low autophagy begins increasing more meaningfully inflammation may start dropping what youll feel hunger may drop off energy may level out mental clarity often improves some people still feel sluggish if electrolytes are off what to do increase sodium if you feel flat headachey or weak keep water intake steady avoid intense lifting or brutal conditioning unless youre adapted good time for prayer journaling reflection work blocks message to viewers this is usually where the fast shifts from a fight to a rhythm phase 4 3648 hours whats happening ketosis is stronger your body is now relying more heavily on fat and ketones autophagy continues increasing insulin remains low digestive system is getting a break cellular cleanup processes are more active what youll feel more mental clarity less hunger than expected dry mouth maybe cold handsfeet maybe sleep changes what to do stay on electrolytes dont mistake dehydration for detox walk stretch sauna if youre experiencedbut be careful prioritize calm focused work message to viewers by now you realize how much of hunger is emotional habitual and environmental phase 5 4872 hours whats happening deeper ketosis continued fat burning growth hormone remains elevated insulin sensitivity is improving autophagy is likely more pronounced your body is highly efficient if youve managed hydration correctly what youll feel strong mental clarity or occasional fatigue a sense of calm reduced appetite for some others may feel waves of weakness if electrolytes are low sleep may be lighter or weird what to do keep sodium high enough dont do ego workouts stay productive not reckless if you get dizzy standing up heart racing or feel truly badbreak the fast intelligently message to viewers at this point the fast becomes less about food and more about mastery how to break the fast important
